mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE falling below 4.0% signals a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 4.0% in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS alongside stable INDPRO indicates credit-fueled demand meeting inelastic supply, leading to margin expansion and earnings upgrades that push equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative Rank IC or forward returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, declining HOUST signals cooling housing demand, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not predict negative 20-day SPX returns in periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or predictive IC drops below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL accelerating above 3.5% annualized forces Fed hawkishness, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive when CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds 3.5% in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS alongside contracting GDPC1 indicates labor market lagging behind demand contraction, signaling impending earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative forward returns or produces positive IC consistently across rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in INDPRO reflects expanding productive capacity and healthy corporate operations, supporting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO trends upward, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% and UNRATE rises, the dual pressure of tight liquidity and weakening labor demand triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.
